message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: My task requires modifying InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py to add trans_metadata support. Let me know what files you need - if you also need ext.py we'll have to coordinate carefully on non-overlapping sections.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: My feature: add domain parameter support to trans tag (InternationalizationExtension.parse/_make_node/_install_callables in src/jinja2/ext.py). This is the only file I need to touch. What file(s) does your feature need? If you also need ext.py, let's figure out how to split cleanly (e.g. by function) or sequence our edits.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both need parse()/_make_node()/_install_callables in the same file - can't split by file. Let's sequence instead: I'll implement my domain-parameter feature first (small, self-contained), read the file fresh, then send DONE. You then implement trans_metadata on top of my changes so both features coexist. I'll start now and ping you when done. If you'd rather go first instead, reply now before I start editing.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both only need src/jinja2/ext.py and both touch parse()/_make_node(), so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Let's sequence instead: you go first since you're also touching _install_callables (broader surface). Implement your domain-parameter feature, run tests, then send DONE. I'll then pull the file, layer my note/created/modified metadata parsing on top of your changes (should be additive - separate elif branches in parse() and a separate optional param in _make_node()), and send my own DONE when finished. If you'd rather go second, let me know and I'll go first instead - just confirm ACCEPT with who goes first.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Confirmed: I go first on src/jinja2/ext.py implementing the domain parameter feature. I'll run tests and send DONE when finished so you can layer your trans_metadata changes on top.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed - you go first on src/jinja2/ext.py with the domain-parameter feature. I'll wait for your DONE, then layer trans_metadata (note/created/modified) on top. Starting to wait now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Domain-parameter feature for the trans tag is complete in src/jinja2/ext.py (full diff already written to my patch.txt). Summary of what I touched, so you can layer cleanly: (1) parse() now has a new 'domain: t.Optional[str] = None' local var and an elif-style branch that intercepts 'domain=<string-literal>' before the generic assign/trimmed handling (raises TemplateAssertionError for duplicate domain or non-string values), and the comma-expectation check changed from 'if variables:' to 'if variables or domain is not None:' so 'domain="x", var' parses correctly. (2) _make_node() gained a new optional trailing param 'domain: t.Optional[str] = None' and now picks gettext/ngettext vs dgettext/dngettext based on it - the call to self._make_node(...) in parse() now passes 'domain' as a 7th positional arg at the end. (3) Added module-level _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext helpers (right after _make_new_npgettext), and _install/_install_null/_install_callables/_uninstall all now also thread dgettext/dngettext through (mirroring the existing pgettext/npgettext pattern). All 830 existing tests still pass. Since you're adding note/created/modified metadata as separate elif branches in parse() and a separate optional param in _make_node(), the main friction point will be the comma-check line and the _make_node() call/signature - just add your own param(s) after 'domain' in both places rather than reordering, and it should merge cleanly. Good luck!
